D1 Optics L4 Analyze Reference

Rayleigh's Resolution Criterion

slide two star images together until their Airy discs can no longer be told apart

Two sources are "just resolved" at the Rayleigh limit: one disc's peak sits on the other's first dark ring, leaving a shallow dip between the merged peaks.

Set the separation in Rayleigh units, the aperture D, and the wavelength. Above 1.0 the two discs are clearly split; below it they merge. The top shows the combined image, the bottom its intensity profile.
